outcome_text: AI system performance or assurance criteria are measured qualitatively or quantitatively and demonstrated for conditions similar to deployment conditions. Measurements are documented.
scoring_text: Skill in AI system performance measurement under deployment-representative conditions, including designing qualitative and quantitative performance assessments, establishing assurance criteria, conducting measurements in simulated deployment environments, and documenting measurement results and conditions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI system performance measurement under deployment-representative conditions, including designing qualitative and quantitative performance assessments, establishing assurance criteria, conducting measurements in simulated deployment environments, and documenting measurement results and conditions.
reference_document_element_description: Operate continuous evaluation processes for deployed AI systems, monitoring for performance degradation, behavioral drift, and emerging failure modes.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.3 (MEASURE) superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.576, cosine: 0.705). Secondary: AI-T-007 (AI, cosine: 0.680), RM-T-003 (RM, cosine: 0.679).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.576). Shared vocabulary: [ai, performance]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assessments, assurance, conditions, conducting, criteria, deployment] vs WIDAI terms [behavioral, continuous, degradation, deployed, drift, emerging] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.873 (P=0.861, R=0.886). R exceeds P by 0.025 — the WIDAI KSA's 18 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 31 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.705 > STS 0.576 (gap +0.129). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, performance] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.7%. Shared tokens: [ai, performance]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assessments, assurance, conditions, conducting, criteria, deployment]. WIDAI-only: [behavioral, continuous, degradation, deployed, drift, emerging].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.129 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
